user_message: The CSA escalated Coho Winery to me -- can you prepare the forecast?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 15:59 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 10:46 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 09:58 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Coho Winery

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into migration architecture.

[00:05:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:07:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:11:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:24:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
